questions.prompt: Some people believe that students should be allowed to use artificial intelligence tools for brainstorming, outlining, or checking grammar as long as they do not submit AI-generated work as their own. Others argue that using these tools too often may weaken students’ ability to think independently and develop original ideas. Schools and colleges must decide how to teach responsible use of new technology while protecting academic honesty. Assignment: Write an essay of 300–600 words in which you develop your point of view on the issue presented in the passage. Support your position with reasoning and examples from your reading, studies, experience, or observations.
questions.modelAnswer: A strong response should state a clear position on student use of artificial intelligence tools, support that position with specific reasons and examples, address both benefits and risks when appropriate, and demonstrate organized, controlled writing.
questions.scoringGuidance: A strong response should state a clear position on student use of artificial intelligence tools, support that position with specific reasons and examples, address both benefits and risks when appropriate, and demonstrate organized, controlled writing.
questions.rubric: Scores range from 8 for a focused, well-developed, logically organized essay with strong language control to 0 for a blank, off-topic, too short, incoherent, or non-English response.
